meta.purpose: Search / agent search benchmark registry for a multilingual search API team. Designed to migrate into a database and power a Chinese frontend.
meta.selection_rule: A benchmark is included when search, retrieval, browsing, tool discovery, or evidence discovery materially affects the score or is useful as a design pattern for evaluating search APIs, search+content APIs, or agent search products.
meta.quality_boundary: This file is a first-pass map based on public pages, papers, repositories, and dataset pages. It is not yet a per-benchmark deep audit. Each benchmark should later receive a verification state after reading the paper, inspecting data files, checking license, and running a minimal sample if possible.

primary_views: benchmark list with filters by family, product line, language, live_web, public data, and automation feasibility
benchmark detail page with task design, dataset design, search dependency, metrics, links, and update history
comparison matrix for product relevance and automation readiness
candidate review queue for newly discovered benchmarks
front_end_notes.database_design_warning: Do not store taxonomy only as markdown text. Keep tags, links, datasets, tasks, metrics, and update events as normalized entities or JSONB columns so the frontend can filter and diff weekly updates.

benchmarks.name: FACTS Search

search_dependency.replaceable_search_api: conceptually yes, but official leaderboard fixes one search engine for fairness
search_dependency.search_output_expected: web search results appended to model context
search_dependency.why_it_matters: It explicitly evaluates whether a model can use Search as a tool to retrieve information and synthesize factual answers.

task_design.process: model may call a search API during answer generation
task_design.search_pattern: tool call selection, query formulation, evidence synthesis

dataset.size: 1842 questions reported by Kaggle snippets
languages: not fully specified in public snippets
dataset.corpus_type: live web through a fixed search API in official evaluation
dataset.public_data: public examples available; full benchmark has public/private leaderboard splits

evaluation.judge: automated judge models in FACTS suite
evaluation.reproducibility: medium; search provider is fixed in official evaluation

automation.can_auto_run: potentially, if benchmark access and search tool protocol are available

benchmarks.notes: High-priority because search is not merely precomputed evidence; it is a first-class tool in the evaluation.

benchmarks.name: BrowseComp

search_dependency.replaceable_search_api: yes for local evaluations; official comparisons may vary by tool stack
search_dependency.search_output_expected: search results plus pages read by the agent
search_dependency.why_it_matters: Questions are designed to require persistent web search for hard-to-find short answers.

task_design.input: short-answer questions reverse-engineered to be hard to locate
task_design.process: agent searches, reformulates queries, reads pages, reconciles evidence
task_design.search_pattern: multi-hop lookup, long-tail source discovery, persistence

dataset.size: 1266 questions
dataset.public_data: available through OpenAI simple-evals / Kaggle references
dataset.update_pattern: static dataset; ecosystem leaderboards update separately

evaluation.reproducibility: medium; live web changes and benchmark contamination are known risks

automation.can_auto_run: yes, with an agent harness and search provider abstraction

benchmarks.notes: Core benchmark for AI-facing search API positioning, but live web variability makes it less ideal for controlled retriever comparisons.

benchmarks.name: BrowseComp-ZH

search_dependency.replaceable_search_api: yes for local evaluations
search_dependency.search_output_expected: Chinese web search results and source pages
search_dependency.why_it_matters: Tests agentic search in Chinese-language web ecosystems, not only translated English tasks.

task_design.process: agent searches Chinese web sources and integrates facts
task_design.search_pattern: Chinese query formulation, entity aliasing, multi-source reconciliation

dataset.size: 289 questions
dataset.public_data: released by authors / mirrored in benchmark platforms

automation.can_auto_run: yes, with Chinese web search support

benchmarks.notes: Important for multilingual product narrative because it exposes non-English web infrastructure and language-specific search difficulty.

benchmarks.name: LiveNewsBench

search_dependency.replaceable_search_api: yes in local evaluations
search_dependency.search_output_expected: fresh search results and page visits
search_dependency.why_it_matters: It is designed to separate parametric memory from live search by generating questions from recent news.

task_design.process: agent performs search, visits pages, and reasons over new information
task_design.output: answer to a recent, often multi-hop question
task_design.search_pattern: freshness, time-sensitive source discovery, multi-hop news lookup

dataset.size: release-based train/validation/test/human-verified splits
languages: Portuguese mentioned in related search results; verify exact release coverage

evaluation.judge: automated evaluation plus human-verified test subset
evaluation.reproducibility: medium-high because data releases are versioned

automation.can_auto_run: yes, strong candidate for scheduled eval

benchmarks.notes: High-priority for search API because freshness is a direct API differentiator.

benchmarks.name: Search Arena

search_dependency.replaceable_search_api: not directly; it is primarily a dataset of interactions and preferences
search_dependency.why_it_matters: It shows real user intents, citations, source preferences, and grounding gaps in search-augmented LLMs.

task_design.input: real user prompts and multi-turn interactions
task_design.process: search-augmented systems respond and users vote
task_design.search_pattern: citation behavior, source credibility, user preference under search augmentation

dataset.size: over 24000 paired multi-turn interactions
dataset.live_web: captured traces rather than rerun live web

automation.can_auto_run: analysis refresh yes; search API eval no

benchmarks.notes: Do not treat as direct search API eval; treat as product research for what AI users ask and how citations affect trust.

benchmarks.name: BrowseComp-Plus
benchmarks.maintaining_org: University of Waterloo / Texttron / Tevatron contributors

search_dependency.replaceable_search_api: yes if exposed as retriever/search tool over the fixed corpus
search_dependency.search_output_expected: retrieved web documents or chunks from a fixed corpus
search_dependency.why_it_matters: It was built to isolate retriever contribution from LLM agent reasoning in deep research tasks.

task_design.process: agent searches a fixed curated corpus, reasons over evidence, answers
task_design.output: short answer with evidence/citation analysis possible
task_design.search_pattern: retriever quality, reranking, search efficiency, evidence recall

dataset.size: 830 queries and roughly 100K curated web documents reported by public summaries
dataset.corpus_type: fixed curated web corpus with supporting docs and hard negatives
dataset.public_data: Hugging Face dataset and GitHub available

automation.can_auto_run: yes, very strong candidate for automated internal benchmark

benchmarks.notes: Especially relevant to a search+content product because it can evaluate retrieval, content chunks, and agent outcomes under a controlled corpus.

benchmarks.name: XBCP / Cross-lingual BrowseComp-Plus

search_dependency.replaceable_search_api: yes if search supports multilingual retrieval over corpus
search_dependency.why_it_matters: It varies supporting document languages to test cross-lingual evidence retrieval and integration.

task_design.input: English questions with evidence in other languages
task_design.process: retriever finds cross-lingual evidence, agent integrates it
task_design.search_pattern: cross-lingual retrieval, evidence recall, multilingual citation fidelity

dataset.size: derived from BrowseComp-Plus; exact release details require follow-up

benchmarks.notes: Candidate to track because it directly connects multilingual search quality to agentic deep research outcomes.

benchmarks.name: AssistantBench

search_dependency.replaceable_search_api: partially; agents may use search plus browsing
search_dependency.search_output_expected: web pages, business listings, sources across sites
search_dependency.why_it_matters: Tasks require locating real information on the open web and often cannot be solved from model memory.

task_design.process: agent browses websites and collects relevant facts
task_design.search_pattern: local/business discovery, monitoring, multi-site information lookup

dataset.size: 214 tasks, more than 525 pages from 258 websites

evaluation.reproducibility: medium due to live web drift

automation.can_auto_run: yes but brittle against web drift

benchmarks.notes: Useful to understand end-user assistant tasks that create demand for search+content beyond plain SERP.

benchmarks.name: GAIA

search_dependency.replaceable_search_api: partially; GAIA also needs files, multimodal reasoning, tools
search_dependency.why_it_matters: Many tasks require web browsing and tool use, but search is not isolated from other capabilities.

task_design.input: real-world assistant questions with unambiguous answers
task_design.process: agent uses browsing, tools, multimodal inputs, and reasoning
task_design.search_pattern: multi-step factual lookup mixed with tool use

dataset.size: 466 questions in paper; public validation and hidden leaderboard splits

automation.can_auto_run: yes for public split, but not search-specific

benchmarks.notes: Good benchmark to track because agent products quote it, but it should be tagged as mixed-capability rather than pure search.

benchmarks.name: WebArena

search_dependency.replaceable_search_api: limited; it is more about web interaction than standalone search API
search_dependency.search_output_expected: self-hosted website pages and environment state
search_dependency.why_it_matters: It evaluates agents converting natural language instructions into web interactions; search/navigation is part of the environment.

task_design.process: agent navigates self-hosted realistic websites
task_design.search_pattern: in-site search, navigation, tool/knowledge base use

dataset.size: benchmark tasks over e-commerce, forum, software development, CMS domains
dataset.update_pattern: family has extensions such as VisualWebArena and WebArena-Infinity

benchmarks.notes: More relevant to agent browser/navigation products than pure search APIs.

benchmarks.name: WebWalkerQA

search_dependency.replaceable_search_api: partially; focuses on traversal inside websites after source discovery
search_dependency.why_it_matters: It tests whether agents can traverse subpages systematically, addressing shallow search result limitations.

task_design.input: question tied to a website/webpage collection
task_design.process: agent traverses links and accumulates evidence
task_design.search_pattern: vertical exploration, link traversal, content extraction

dataset.size: 680 queries across four scenarios and over 1373 webpages
languages: English plus possible multilingual distribution; verify release

automation.can_auto_run: yes if data access is stable

benchmarks.notes: Useful for search+content APIs that plan to return not only a page but crawled/structured subpage content.

benchmarks.name: WideSearch

search_dependency.replaceable_search_api: yes in local evals
search_dependency.search_output_expected: many atomic facts from diverse web sources
search_dependency.why_it_matters: It targets broad, exhaustive information collection where recall and completeness dominate.

task_design.input: large-scale collection question with expected table/structured output
task_design.process: agent gathers many atomic facts and fills an organized output
task_design.search_pattern: broad coverage, deduplication, completeness, verification

dataset.size: 200 tasks, 100 English and 100 Chinese, over 15 domains

evaluation.reproducibility: medium due to open web dependency

benchmarks.notes: Excellent product-design signal for agent search where users need exhaustive collection rather than one best answer.

benchmarks.name: SGR-Bench

search_dependency.replaceable_search_api: partially; requires source discovery plus site-specific state configuration
search_dependency.search_output_expected: structured answers from specialized public data ecosystems
search_dependency.why_it_matters: It isolates a common failure mode: finding a relevant source but using the wrong filter/view/scope.

task_design.process: agent discovers correct site and configures filters/hierarchies/views
task_design.search_pattern: state-gated retrieval, query constraints, filter accuracy

dataset.size: 100 expert-curated tasks across six source families and 12 public data ecosystems
dataset.public_data: Hugging Face dataset and single-case instructions

evaluation.reproducibility: medium; full evaluation infra may be separated

automation.can_auto_run: pending infra access; strong candidate for future agent search eval

benchmarks.notes: Very relevant to agent search products that need to search within structured, filter-heavy websites rather than only retrieve documents.

benchmarks.name: DeepSearchQA

search_dependency.replaceable_search_api: likely yes in local harnesses; official setup should be verified
search_dependency.search_output_expected: multi-step search results and evidence for exhaustive answer lists
search_dependency.why_it_matters: It explicitly evaluates agents on difficult multi-step information-seeking tasks requiring comprehensive answer sets.

task_design.process: agent plans searches, identifies gaps, searches again, compiles exhaustive results
task_design.search_pattern: causal-chain search, comprehensiveness, recall over multiple fields

dataset.size: 900 prompts across 17 fields
dataset.public_data: Kaggle benchmark and Hugging Face dataset references

benchmarks.notes: High-priority for future agent search because it stresses comprehensive search planning rather than one-shot lookup.

benchmarks.name: DeepResearch Bench

search_dependency.replaceable_search_api: partially; full deep research systems include many components
search_dependency.search_output_expected: cited sources and generated research report
search_dependency.why_it_matters: It evaluates information collection and citation trustworthiness as part of deep research reports.

task_design.process: agent searches, collects sources, synthesizes long report
task_design.search_pattern: multi-step exploration, source collection, citation support

dataset.size: 100 tasks, 50 English and 50 Chinese, across 22 domains
dataset.public_data: query data, raw model outputs, evaluation framework
dataset.update_pattern: actively maintained with DRB II follow-up

evaluation.reproducibility: medium; judge models and scraping dependencies matter

automation.can_auto_run: yes but costly; useful after harness maturity

benchmarks.notes: Better for product-level deep research agents than for isolating search API quality.

benchmarks.name: Deep Research Bench (FutureSearch / RetroSearch)

search_dependency.replaceable_search_api: yes if API can search the frozen RetroSearch corpus
search_dependency.search_output_expected: retrieved frozen webpages and long agent traces
search_dependency.why_it_matters: It controls web drift with a frozen scraped web environment, making repeated evaluations more reliable.

task_design.process: agent searches frozen pages or live web equivalent, reasons, answers
task_design.search_pattern: offline search over 10K-100K pages per task, hallucination/tool-use trace analysis

dataset.size: 89 tasks in paper; public eval pages mention evolving task counts

evaluation.reproducibility: high relative to live web benchmarks

automation.can_auto_run: pending environment access; strong architecture reference

benchmarks.notes: Important design pattern for building a stable internal benchmark: freeze web snapshots while preserving search-like interaction.

benchmarks.name: AgentSearchBench

search_dependency.replaceable_search_api: yes, for agent retrieval and reranking experiments
search_dependency.search_output_expected: ranked candidate agents for a task
search_dependency.why_it_matters: It formalizes agent search as retrieval/reranking with execution-grounded relevance, directly matching future agent search products.

task_design.input: executable task queries and high-level task descriptions
task_design.process: system retrieves/reranks candidate agents
task_design.search_pattern: semantic retrieval, reranking, behavioral/execution-aware signals

dataset.size: nearly 10000 real-world agents across providers
dataset.corpus_type: agent descriptions, generated tasks, execution-derived labels

evaluation.judge: LLM judge plus quality controls and human alignment validation

automation.can_auto_run: yes, strongest direct candidate for agent search

benchmarks.notes: Top-priority for the planned agent search product line.

benchmarks.name: AgentSelect

search_dependency.replaceable_search_api: yes for recommendation/retrieval modeling
search_dependency.search_output_expected: recommended agent configurations for narrative queries
search_dependency.why_it_matters: It reframes agent selection as query-to-agent recommendation over a very large candidate pool.

task_design.process: system matches query to deployable agent/profile/configuration
task_design.search_pattern: long-tail recommendation, capability matching, transfer to marketplaces

dataset.size: 111179 queries, 107721 deployable agents, 251103 interaction records aggregated from 40+ sources
dataset.corpus_type: agent profiles and heterogeneous evaluation-derived interaction signals

evaluation.judge: positive-only interaction data and benchmark-specific evaluation

benchmarks.notes: Complementary to AgentSearchBench. More recommendation-flavored and broader in candidate scale.

benchmarks.name: AutoResearchBench

search_dependency.replaceable_search_api: yes if search indexes the scientific paper corpus
search_dependency.why_it_matters: It evaluates autonomous literature discovery, a domain where search quality directly controls success.

task_design.process: agent searches, browses, reasons over papers
task_design.output: target paper or complete set of qualifying papers
task_design.search_pattern: deep target discovery and wide set collection in academic corpora

dataset.size: 1000 curated questions, 195 CS topics, over 50000 papers reported by project page

automation.can_auto_run: yes if corpus/index setup is available

benchmarks.notes: A vertical search-agent benchmark. Useful as a pattern for domain-specific agent search APIs.

benchmarks.name: BEIR

search_dependency.replaceable_search_api: yes for corpus retrieval experiments
search_dependency.why_it_matters: It is a standard heterogeneous IR benchmark for retrieval models across domains.

task_design.process: retrieve ranked documents from fixed corpora
task_design.search_pattern: zero-shot domain generalization

dataset.size: 18 diverse datasets in original paper; 15+ in repo summaries

evaluation.judge: human relevance labels from component datasets

benchmarks.notes: Good for retriever baselines, but not enough to evaluate web freshness, crawling, snippet quality, or agent search behavior.

benchmarks.name: MTEB Retrieval

search_dependency.replaceable_search_api: yes for embedding/reranking components
search_dependency.why_it_matters: It is the dominant public leaderboard for embedding models, including retrieval and multilingual retrieval tasks.

task_design.input: query or text input depending on task
task_design.process: retrieve, rerank, classify, cluster, etc.; focus here is retrieval subset
task_design.search_pattern: embedding model comparison and multilingual coverage

dataset.size: many tasks across languages; exact selection depends on leaderboard version

benchmarks.notes: Useful for model/component selection; should not be confused with an end-to-end web search API benchmark.

benchmarks.name: MS MARCO

search_dependency.replaceable_search_api: yes for passage/document ranking over dataset
search_dependency.why_it_matters: It is a foundational search ranking dataset derived from real Bing queries and web passages/documents.

task_design.process: rank passages/documents from fixed corpus
task_design.search_pattern: large-scale supervised web ranking

dataset.size: 8.8M passages; hundreds of thousands of training queries depending on task/version
languages: primarily English; web search variants include broader language metadata
dataset.update_pattern: mature/partly retired leaderboards, v2 variants exist

benchmarks.notes: Baseline search relevance benchmark; useful for ranking but not agentic behavior.

benchmarks.name: TREC Deep Learning Track

search_dependency.replaceable_search_api: yes for ranking systems
search_dependency.why_it_matters: It provides deeper judged test collections around MS MARCO-style retrieval.

task_design.process: full retrieval or reranking
task_design.search_pattern: ad hoc retrieval in large-data regime

dataset.size: year-specific judged query sets over MS MARCO collections

benchmarks.notes: Important for rigorous IR methodology and metrics.

benchmarks.name: MIRACL

search_dependency.replaceable_search_api: yes for multilingual retrieval components
search_dependency.why_it_matters: It provides high-quality human relevance judgments across 18 languages.

task_design.process: retrieve relevant passages in same language
task_design.search_pattern: monolingual multilingual retrieval quality

dataset.size: over 726K relevance judgments for 78K queries across 18 languages reported by paper/source pages

benchmarks.notes: Core multilingual retrieval benchmark; limited to Wikipedia-style corpus.

benchmarks.name: TREC NeuCLIR / NeuCLIRBench

search_dependency.replaceable_search_api: yes for cross-language retrieval systems
search_dependency.search_output_expected: ranked multilingual / cross-language news or technical documents
search_dependency.why_it_matters: It studies cross-language and multilingual retrieval, including report generation variants.

task_design.input: topics in one language querying documents in another/multiple languages
task_design.process: cross-language retrieval and sometimes generation
task_design.search_pattern: CLIR, MLIR, multilingual news and technical search

dataset.size: year-specific TREC collections; NeuCLIRBench aggregates track queries
dataset.public_data: track data and HF benchmark references

benchmarks.notes: Good for global/multilingual search API research, especially cross-language behavior.

benchmarks.name: Mr. TyDi

search_dependency.replaceable_search_api: yes for retrieval components
search_dependency.why_it_matters: It evaluates monolingual dense retrieval across 11 typologically diverse languages.

task_design.process: retrieve relevant passages in same language
task_design.search_pattern: non-English dense retrieval generalization

dataset.size: 11 languages; dataset derived from TyDi

benchmarks.notes: Good component benchmark for multilingual retrieval; less directly tied to web search UX.

benchmarks.name: FRAMES

search_dependency.replaceable_search_api: yes if the system retrieves from the required corpus; not a live web API benchmark
search_dependency.why_it_matters: It evaluates factuality, retrieval, and reasoning jointly for multi-hop RAG questions.

task_design.process: retrieve needed sources and reason over them
task_design.search_pattern: retrieval completeness, temporal disambiguation, multi-document synthesis

dataset.size: 824 questions

benchmarks.notes: Include as a RAG design pattern, not as proof of live search API quality.

benchmarks.name: MultiHop-RAG

search_dependency.replaceable_search_api: yes over fixed knowledge base
search_dependency.why_it_matters: It isolates multi-hop retrieval and reasoning failures in RAG pipelines.

task_design.process: retrieve evidence across 2-4 documents and answer
task_design.search_pattern: multi-document evidence retrieval, metadata filtering

dataset.size: 2556 queries

benchmarks.notes: Useful for testing processed content and evidence retrieval.

benchmarks.name: HotpotQA
benchmarks.maintaining_org: CMU / Stanford / Universite de Montreal / Google AI authors

search_dependency.replaceable_search_api: yes for open-domain variants; many setups provide context
search_dependency.why_it_matters: It is a classic multi-hop QA dataset with supporting facts, useful for evidence retrieval patterns.

task_design.process: find and reason over multiple supporting documents/facts
task_design.search_pattern: multi-hop evidence lookup

dataset.size: 113K Wikipedia-based QA pairs

benchmarks.notes: Legacy pattern benchmark; less directly relevant to modern web search APIs.

benchmarks.name: Natural Questions

search_dependency.replaceable_search_api: partially; original annotation used Google top results but benchmark is mostly fixed QA
search_dependency.why_it_matters: Questions are real Google queries, and annotation was based on top search-result Wikipedia pages.

task_design.process: read retrieved Wikipedia page and identify answer span
task_design.output: long answer and short answer or null
task_design.search_pattern: question answering from search-result pages

dataset.size: over 300K training examples, plus dev/test sets
dataset.corpus_type: Wikipedia pages selected from search results

benchmarks.notes: Useful historical source for query intent and answer extraction, but not an agentic search benchmark.

benchmarks.name: AIMultiple Agentic Search API Benchmark

search_dependency.replaceable_search_api: yes; it directly compares search APIs
search_dependency.why_it_matters: It directly benchmarks search APIs for AI/LLM queries, but methodology must be audited.

task_design.input: 100 real-world AI/LLM queries according to article
task_design.process: query multiple search APIs and score retrieved results
task_design.search_pattern: result relevance, quality, noise, source type, latency/cost depending on article

dataset.size: 100 queries and 4000 retrieved results reported
dataset.public_data: methodology/results article; raw dataset availability unclear

evaluation.reproducibility: low until raw queries/judging prompts are available

benchmarks.notes: Include as market signal, not as trusted scientific benchmark unless raw methodology is reviewed.

benchmarks.name: Parallel Quality Benchmarks / SealQA references

search_dependency.replaceable_search_api: yes in principle, but vendor methodology must be separated from product marketing
search_dependency.why_it_matters: Vendor benchmarks often reflect buyer-facing claims about AI-native search API accuracy and price-performance.

task_design.process: compare web search offerings or task APIs
task_design.search_pattern: answer accuracy under web search infrastructure

dataset.size: varies by benchmark; SealQA references public/private details should be verified

automation.can_auto_run: no, unless independent harness is built

benchmarks.notes: Track as competitive intelligence and benchmark-design inspiration, not as neutral evidence.
